notes: Arturia Augmented BRASS is a hybrid instrument combining recorded brass ensemble samples with a synthesis layer, enabling a range from realistic orchestral brass to full electronic textures with brass tonality woven in. The acoustic layer captures the attack transient, valve noise, air, and timbral richness of real brass sections, while the synth layer adds sustain, pads, and electronic colour. The blend is continuously controllable via the Morph control, making it ideal for transitions from acoustic section writing to electronic sound design within a single patch. Attack shaping is particularly important as it defines whether the brass feels live and punchy or smooth and synthetic.

Expression.description: Expression — scales output level; essential for brass dynamics and crescendos

Modulation.description: Blend/Morph — continuously sweeps between the Acoustic brass layer and the Synth layer

Acoustic Layer.description: Acoustic brass layer level — level of the real brass ensemble recordings

Synth Layer.description: Synth layer level — level of the synthesis engine

Filter Cutoff.description: Filter cutoff — shapes brightness; opening the filter emphasises the bright, cutting character of brass

Filter Resonance.description: Filter resonance — adds tonal emphasis at cutoff

Attack.description: Amplitude envelope attack — short attack = punchy brass stabs; long attack = slow swells for film scoring

Release.description: Amplitude envelope release — tail length after note off

Reverb.description: Reverb send level — concert hall or stage reverb sits the brass in an acoustic space

Motion Rate.description: Motion/LFO rate — automated movement across the parameter space for evolving textures

Motion Depth.description: Motion/LFO depth — amount of automated movement
